title: My Homework Ate Itself
lyrics:
[verse]
My homework ate itself last night
I left it on my desk, it took a bite
My pencil ran away at two AM
My eraser said it's done, it quit, the end

[chorus]
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
My homework ate itself
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
It's sitting on the shelf
Looking full, looking pleased
My homework ate itself, oh well

[verse]
My backpack said it needs a holiday
My textbook fell asleep on chapter eight
The Wi-Fi works for everyone but me
My laptop screen just shows a cup of tea

[chorus]
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
My homework ate itself
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
It's sitting on the shelf
Looking full, looking pleased
My homework ate itself, oh well

[bridge]
The teacher says I need to try much harder
My notebook says it's doing all it can
My desk suggested maybe we should all
Just take a nap and try again

[chorus]
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
My homework ate itself
It's not my fault, it's not my fault
Goodnight, homework
See you tomorrow
You'll probably eat yourself again
style_prompt: indie pop, gentle sarcasm, acoustic guitar, soft piano, light drums, 100 BPM, young female vocal, understated, slightly bored delivery, witty, dry, cool and unbothered, like a tired student who finds everything mildly absurd
cover_description: A pre-teen girl sitting at a messy desk looking unbothered, a piece of paper with a bite taken out of it, a pencil running away in the background, a laptop showing a teacup on screen, soft bedroom lighting
